title: Glutathione Assay Protocol
author: Peter Havel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ykcfusw
materials: Cover the plate with the plate cover provided.

steps.title: Add 50 µl of Standard (tubes...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl of Standard (tubes A-H ) per well in the designated wells on the plate (see Sample Plate Format , Figure 2, page 13). Figure 2. Sample plate format

steps.title: Add 50 µl of sample to...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µl of sample to each of the sample wells.

steps.title: Prepare the Assay Cocktail by mixing...
steps.instruction: Prepare the Assay Cocktail by mixing the following reagents in a 20 ml vial: MES Buffer (11.25 ml), reconstituted Cofactor Mixture (0.45 ml), reconstituted Enzyme Mixture (2.1 ml), water (2.3 ml), and reconstituted DTNB (0.45 ml). NOTE: The volumes of reagents given are for the use of the entire plate. Adjust the volumes of the reagents accordingly if only a part of the plate is used. Prepare fresh Assay Cocktail and run a standard curve each time the assay is performed. Use the Assay Cocktail within 10 minutes of preparation.

steps.title: Remove the plate cover and add...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate cover and add 150 µl of the freshly prepared Assay Cocktail to each of the wells containing standards and samples using a multichannel pipette. Replace the plate cover and incubate the plate in the dark on an orbital shaker.

steps.title: Measure the absorbance in the wells...
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance in the wells at 405-414 nm using a plate reader at five minutes intevals for 30 minutes (a total of 6 measurements). NOTE: If only the end point method of calculation (see page 18) is used, one measurement at 25 minutes in enough. Expected absorbance of the lowest standard (standard A) at 405 nm is 0.15-0.25 AU and that of the highest standard (standard H) is 0.6-0.8 AU in 30 minutes.

steps.title: Calculate the average absorbance from the...
steps.instruction: Calculate the average absorbance from the 25 minutes measurement for each standard and sample.

steps.title: Subtract the absorbance value of the...
steps.instruction: Subtract the absorbance value of the standard A from itself and all other values (both standards and samples). This is the corrected absorbance.

steps.title: Plot the corrected absorbance values (from...
steps.instruction: Plot the corrected absorbance values (from step 2 above) of each standard as a function of the concentration of GSSG or Total GSH of Table 1 (see Figure 3, on page 18). Table

steps.title: Glutathione standards *under the assay conditions...
steps.instruction: Glutathione standards *under the assay conditions GSSG is reduced to produce 2 mole equivalents of GSH. Figure

steps.title: Plot of corrected absorbance at 25...
steps.instruction: Plot of corrected absorbance at 25 minutes versus GSSG concentration (µM)

steps.title: Calculate the values of GSSG or...
steps.instruction: Calculate the values of GSSG or Total GSH for each sample from the standard curve. NOTE: If your sample required deproteination, multiply by "2" to account for the addition of MPA Reagent.
